data.title: Significant Inflows Recorded in Solana Spot ETFs
data.description: According to data from SoSoValue, on February 19, Solana (SOL) spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $5.9436 million. According to ChainCatcher, the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) led the inflows with $5.4618 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $691 million. The Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) followed with a net inflow of $481,800, reaching a historical total of $160 million. As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs stands at $710 million, with a net asset ratio of 1.52% and cumulative historical net inflows amounting to $886 million.

data.title: Hong Kong Aims to Become International Gold Trading Hub
data.description:
Hong Kong's Deputy Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Chan Ho-lim, highlighted the impact of geopolitical uncertainties on the global environment over the past year. According to RTHK, despite these challenges, Hong Kong's asset market has shown improvement with continuous capital inflow, an increase in visitor numbers, stable local consumption, and strong performance in overall exports and fixed capital investment. The economy grew by 3.5% last year, marking the third consecutive year of expansion.

Speaking at the Hong Kong Gold Exchange's Lunar New Year opening ceremony, Chan emphasized the government's efforts to diversify Hong Kong's international financial services. The government is actively promoting Hong Kong as an international gold trading center, aiming to attract gold storage, clearing, and settlement activities. This initiative is expected to boost investment in derivative products, storage, insurance, trade, and logistics sectors.

The government has set a target to exceed 2,000 tons of gold storage within three years, establishing Hong Kong as a regional gold storage hub. Efforts are also underway to encourage gold merchants to build or expand refineries in Hong Kong. A memorandum of cooperation has been signed with the Shenzhen Local Financial Supervision Bureau to support Hong Kong gold merchants in refining gold in Shenzhen for export to Hong Kong for trading and settlement. Additionally, the government plans to enhance cooperation between the Shanghai and Hong Kong gold markets. The Hong Kong Gold Clearing System, fully owned by the government, aims to begin trial operations within this year.

Chan stated that the government will continue to optimize policies, financial infrastructure, and technological innovation to attract more international capital and talent to Hong Kong. These efforts are intended to strengthen Hong Kong's role as a super connector and value-added hub, aligning with the national development strategy outlined in the 15th Five-Year Plan, and reinforcing Hong Kong's status as an international financial center.

data.title: Sharplink's institutional holdings hit a record high, with ETH holdings reaching 867,798.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company Sharplink announced a record high institutional ownership of 46%, along with a rebranding and expanded leadership team. The company also disclosed 867,798 ETH held (including 587,232 native ETH, 225,429 ETH redeemed from LsETH, and 55,137 ETH redeemed from WeETH), and has generated 13,615 ETH in staking rewards since June 2025, all of which have been distributed to shareholders. (Globenewswire)

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Semiconductor Stocks Decline Amid Market Weakness
data.description: Hong Kong's semiconductor sector experienced a downturn as several key stocks saw significant declines. According to Jin10, TianShu Intelligence (09903.HK) and HongGuang Semiconductor (06908.HK) both fell over 5%, while Hua Hong Semiconductor (01347.HK) dropped 4.8%. Additionally, NaXin Microelectronics (02676.HK) and Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (00981.HK) decreased by more than 2%. The market sentiment reflects broader concerns impacting the semiconductor industry.

data.title: Hong Kong Storage Concept Stocks Strengthen as Semiconductor Companies Rise
data.description: Hong Kong's storage concept stocks have shown significant growth, with semiconductor companies leading the charge. According to Jin10, Lam Research Technology (06809.HK) saw an increase of 8%, while Gigadevice Semiconductor (03986.HK) rose by 3.45%. This upward trend reflects a positive sentiment in the market towards storage technology and its potential impact on the industry.

data.title: Metaplanet CEO Addresses Allegations of Dishonest Information Disclosure
data.description:
Metaplanet CEO Simon Gerovich has responded to accusations from an anonymous account on the X platform, which claimed the company was dishonest in its information disclosure. According to ChainCatcher, Gerovich refuted these claims, stating that both he and the company are willing to take public responsibility for their actions. He emphasized that Metaplanet's strategy of systematically increasing its Bitcoin holdings remains unchanged.

Gerovich explained that over the past six months, amid rising volatility, the company has increased its allocation to yield-generating businesses. This was achieved by selling put options and using spread strategies to earn premiums, with some funds directed towards long-term Bitcoin holdings. He assured that all Bitcoin purchases are disclosed immediately after decisions are made, and the company's Bitcoin addresses are publicly available. Shareholders can view holdings through a real-time dashboard.

Addressing allegations of undisclosed high-level purchases in September, Gerovich clarified that four Bitcoin acquisitions were made during that month, all of which were promptly announced. He stressed that the company's strategy is not based on timing the market but on a long-term, systematic accumulation of Bitcoin. Selling put options is not merely a bet on price increases but a method to acquire Bitcoin at an effective cost below the spot price.

Gerovich also noted that recurring losses are primarily due to the unrealized fair value fluctuations of Bitcoin, which the company holds long-term and does not intend to sell. Interpreting these fluctuations as strategic failures is a misunderstanding, he added.

data.title: Tether's Tokenized Gold Transfers Highlight Efficiency in Cross-Border Movement
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino announced on the X platform that over the past six months, tokenized gold equivalent to 94 tons has been transferred on-chain, incurring a total fee of only 0.0016%. According to ChainCatcher, this contrasts sharply with the traditional system where central banks spend millions on logistics and security to transport physical gold. Tokenized gold enables nearly instant settlement and low-cost transfers, showcasing the advantages of on-chain assets in cross-border movement efficiency. XAUT, issued by Tether, is a tokenized asset pegged to physical gold, allowing users to transfer gold ownership on the blockchain.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | France Questions EU Participation in U.S.-Led Peace Committee
data.description: France has expressed surprise over the European Union Commission's decision to send officials to a U.S.-initiated 'Peace Committee' meeting without authorization from the European Council. According to Jin10, French Foreign Ministry spokesperson Anne-Claire Legendre stated on February 19 that the EU official involved should provide an explanation for attending the meeting. Legendre emphasized that France cannot participate in the committee unless its focus is clearly defined on the Gaza situation, highlighting concerns over the committee's ambiguous scope.

data.title: STOCKS | South Korean Brokerage Stock Soars 200% on SpaceX Investment
data.description: A South Korean brokerage firm has experienced a remarkable 200% increase in its stock value, driven by its investment in Elon Musk's SpaceX. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant impact of the brokerage's strategic move. The surge reflects growing investor confidence in SpaceX's potential and the brokerage's decision to capitalize on this opportunity. This development underscores the influence of high-profile tech investments on global markets, particularly in the context of innovative aerospace ventures.

data.title: UAW: Volkswagen Workers Approve Historic Union Contract in Southern U.S.
data.description: Volkswagen workers in the southern United States have made history by approving their first union contract, according to the United Auto Workers (UAW). According to Jin10, the contract was approved with a 96% vote in favor, marking a significant milestone for labor organization in the region's major automotive plants. This development highlights the growing influence of unions in areas traditionally resistant to organized labor. The approval of this contract is seen as a pivotal moment for workers seeking better conditions and representation in the automotive industry.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Predicted to Reach $5,400 per Ounce by 2026
data.description: Goldman Sachs has projected a gradual increase in gold prices, according to PANews. The analysis suggests that central banks' gold purchases and private investors expanding their gold holdings in anticipation of U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate cuts will drive the price of gold to $5,400 per ounce by the end of 2026.

data.title: Bitdeer to raise $300 million through convertible senior notes offering
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer announced it will raise $300 million through a convertible senior note offering, depending on market conditions and other factors. Bitdeer also plans to grant initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $45 million of the notes within 13 days of the initial offering date (inclusive). (Stocktitan)

data.title: Elon Musk's Early Adoption of NVIDIA's AI Supercomputer
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, recently shared an intriguing story about Elon Musk's early involvement with NVIDIA's AI supercomputing technology. Musk, known for his innovative ventures, was reportedly the first customer to purchase NVIDIA's powerful AI supercomputer. This move highlights Musk's foresight in recognizing the potential of AI technology, which has since become integral to various industries. Huang's anecdote underscores the significance of early adoption in technological advancements and the role of visionary leaders like Musk in shaping the future of AI.

data.title: U.S. Dollar softens as NFP guides Fed cut expectations
data.description:
Analysts cite REER/DEER overvaluation tariffs and outflows as fading U. S. exceptionalism meets Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP) shaping Fed cut bets and pressuring USD.
source: https://coincu.com/news/u-s-dollar-softens-as-nfp-guides-fed-cut-expectations/

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Hong Kong Gold Exchange Opens with Higher Prices
data.description:
The Hong Kong Gold Exchange commenced its New Year trading session with an opening ceremony. According to RTHK, the local gold price for 99 gold opened at HKD 46,518 per tael, marking an increase of HKD 93 compared to the last trading day of the Year of the Snake.

Additionally, the exchange showcased nearly 800 gold and silver bars, weighing close to one ton and valued at approximately HKD 200 million.

data.title: Bollywood Spy Thriller 'Dhurandhar' Tops Netflix Charts Amid Nationalism Debate
data.description: The Bollywood spy thriller 'Dhurandhar' has become a top hit on Netflix, gaining popularity on both sides of the India-Pakistan border. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the film's success despite sparking discussions about nationalism and propaganda. The movie's storyline and themes have led to a broader conversation about its impact on viewers in the region. As it continues to attract audiences, the film's reception reflects the complex interplay of entertainment and political narratives in South Asia.

data.title: Former Google Engineers Indicted for Alleged Trade Secret Theft
data.description: Two former Google engineers, along with the husband of one of them, have been indicted for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to Google's Tensor processor used in Pixel phones. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the legal action taken against the individuals. The indictment accuses the trio of unlawfully obtaining confidential information from Google, which is a significant component of the company's smartphone technology. The case underscores the ongoing challenges tech companies face in protecting their proprietary information from internal and external threats. Further details on the legal proceedings and potential consequences for the accused have not been disclosed.

data.title: Anchorage Launches Stablecoin Alternative to Correspondent Banking
data.description:
Anchorage Digital has introduced a new service that enables non-U. S. banks to settle cross-border transactions using regulated stablecoins instead of traditional correspondent banking networks. The initiative targets long-standing inefficiencies tied to the global “de-risking” trend, where major U
source: https://www.ethnews.com/anchorage-launches-stablecoin-alternative-to-correspondent-banking/

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Direct Release of Government Files on Extraterrestrial Life
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to instruct the Secretary of Defense and other relevant departments and agencies to begin identifying and releasing government files related to extraterrestrial life. According to Jin10, this move aims to increase transparency and public access to information concerning alien life and unidentified flying objects. The decision comes amid growing public interest and speculation about the existence of extraterrestrial beings and the government's knowledge of such phenomena. The initiative is expected to shed light on previously classified documents and potentially reveal new insights into the government's investigations and findings on the subject.
